channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: The week in pictures: Ebola outbreak in Africa, Putin in China and Cannes Film Festival mermaids
item.description: The Ebola outbreak spreads in Democratic Republic of Congo, Russian President Vladimir Putin and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping meet in Beijing, Japanese actress Arisa Sasaki dresses as a mermaid for the Cannes Film Festival and Gaza flotilla activists are humiliated by Israel’s far-right National Security Minister Ben-Gvir. Here is a look back at some of the week’s most striking images.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: War in Ukraine: Russia launches massive missile strike on Kyiv
item.description: Macron, EU’s Kallas condemn Russian attack on Ukraine with Oreshnik ballistic missile.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US: Pentagon releases second batch of UFO declassified footage
item.description: The Pentagon releases 64 UFO documents as Trump orders second batch of declassified footage.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: California declares chemical emergency, 40 000 residents ordered out
item.description: About 40,000 Californians were forced to evacuate their homes on Friday following a leak from a chemical storage tank in the southern part of the state. On Saturday, firefighters recorded an increase in the temperature of the hazardous substance, rising from 25°C to 32°C.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Roland-Garros 2026: French Open starts amid heat wave in Paris
item.description: James Vasina is live from Paris.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Train bombing in Pakistan: At least 24 killed, several in critical condition
item.description: Shahzaib Wahlah is live from Islamabad, Pakistan.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Macron, EU’s Kallas condemn Russian attack on Ukraine with Oreshnik ballistic missile
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: More than 20 people killed in suicide bombing targeting Pakistani passenger train
item.description: At least 23 people have been killed after a suicide bomber detonated his explosive-laden car near a railway in the city of Quetta, in southwestern Pakistan, just as a passenger train passed. More than 70 people were also injured in the blast so powerful that two of the train cars overturned and caught fire.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump says Iran deal 'largely negotiated', would reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A "largely negotiated" memorandum of understanding on an Iran peace ​deal would reopen the Strait of Hormuz, U.S. President Donald Trump said on Saturday, as expectations rose that a turning point might be imminent in the three-month-old war.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Protesters clash with police after an anti-government rally in Serbia's capital
item.description: Clashes erupted between groups of protesters and riot police after a huge anti-government rally on Saturday in the Serbian capital of Belgrade by tens of thousands of opponents of the country's autocratic President Aleksandar Vucic.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ten African countries at risk from Ebola virus Africa CDC warns
item.description: Emmet Livingstone is live from Kinshasa, DR Congo.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France bans Israeli minister Itamar Ben-Gvir after 'unspeakable' behavior
item.description: France on Saturday banned Israel’s far-right national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, citing his "unspeakable" behavior targeting activists from a flotilla to Gaza who were detained by his police force.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: War in the Middle East: Trump says Iran deal 'largely negociated'
item.description: Fraser Jackson has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: War in Ukraine: Russian strikes hit a school killing one person
item.description: Gulliver Cragg is live from Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Senegal's president sacks PM: Senegalese Parliament dissolved
item.description: Senegal President Bassirou Diomaye Faye sacked Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko and dissolved the government on Friday after months of tensions, deepening a crisis in the debt-laden west African nation.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US-Cuba: Despite Trump's pressure, Cuba may not turn out like Venezuela
item.description: Ed Augustin has more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Norway-set drama about political polarization ‘Fjord’ wins Palme d’Or at Cannes
item.description: Cristian Mungiu’s Norway-set drama about political polarization, “Fjord,” has won the Palme d’Or, handing the Cannes Film Festival ’s top honor for the second time to Mungiu, the Romanian director of “4 Months, 3 Weeks and 2 Days.”
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Opening Day of Roland Garros: Brazilian prodigy Joao Fronseca to play
item.description: Follow the latest on France 24.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Enhanced Games: The first-ever 'Steroid Olympics' hit Las Vegas
item.description: The first-ever Enhanced Games -- widely dubbed the "Steroid Olympics" -- take place Sunday in Las Vegas, where elite sprinters, swimmers and weightlifters will vie for world records while taking banned performance-enhancing drugs.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Enhanced Games: Steroids, anabolic and other drugs authorized
item.description: Andy Miah, Professor and Bioethicist at University of Salford, is our guest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Premier League: Pep Guardiola set for emotional Manchester City farewell
item.description: After a decade on the Manchester City bench, Pep Guardiola will take charge of his final game for the Citizens this Sunday against Aston Villa. With City guaranteed a second-place finish and the title already out of reach, the pressure is off, but emotions will be running high as an extraordinary era comes to an end.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: US secret service kills gunman near White House, President Trump says suspect had ‘violent history’
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Despite Middle East war, foreign pilgrims flock to Mecca for hajj
item.description: Despite the war in the Middle East, foreign pilgrims have flocked to Mecca to participate in this year’s hajj. According to officials, more than 1.5 million have already arrived in Saudi Arabia, surpassing last year’s numbers.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
